text.title: Multi-Region Active-Active Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of multi-region-active-active-pattern enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every writable data class has an explicit conflict policy (last-writer-wins, vector-clock CRDT, leader-per-key, custom merge) — defaults are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit policy, conflicts get resolved silently and surprise users.

rule.statement: Cross-region replication latency is monitored and treated as an SLI; breach triggers a degraded mode, not silent stale reads.
rule.rationale: Stale reads in active-active are the silent-corruption vector.

rule.statement: Failover drills run quarterly at minimum, with both regions exercised; the drill produces a runbook update.
rule.rationale: Untested failover = no failover; quarterly cadence keeps muscle memory.

rule.statement: Traffic steering at the edge respects residency constraints — e.g. EU users never routed to a non-EU region even on failover.
rule.rationale: Failover that violates residency is a regulatory incident.

rule.statement: Region partition is a tested mode; the system has a defined behaviour for 'lost peer region' (degraded, read-only, paused).
rule.rationale: Untested partition = the system improvises during the actual partition.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
